name: Snowify
description: A desktop music player built with Electron that streams audio from YouTube Music. Clean UI, no accounts, no ads.
readme_summary: Snowify is a desktop music player built using Electron that streams audio directly from YouTube Music. It offers a clean user interface without requiring accounts or displaying ads, providing features like search, playback controls, playlist management, and synced lyrics.
key_features: Search YouTube Music
Stream audio with playback controls
Manage music queues
Create and manage playlists
View synced lyrics
Follow artists and view their pages
Watch music videos
Discord Rich Presence integration
Cloud sync for library
Customizable themes
primary_use_case: To provide a desktop music listening experience by streaming audio from YouTube Music, offering advanced features and a clean interface without ads or account requirements.
cover_image_prompt: A stylized, glowing music note transforming into a clean, modern desktop application window. The music note is composed of digital particles flowing from a stylized YouTube Music logo. Inside the application window, abstract representations of song queues and lyrics are visible as glowing lines of code or musical staves. The background is a dark, minimalist space with subtle hints of sound waves. The overall mood is sleek and futuristic, emphasizing seamless audio streaming and a clutter-free experience. The repository name 'Snowify' is subtly integrated into the application's interface as a title. 3D isometric illustration style with vibrant blue and white accents.

name: autocomplete
description: IDE-style autocomplete for your existing terminal & shell
readme_summary: Amazon Q Developer CLI, formerly Fig, provides IDE-style autocompletion for terminal commands. It enhances the command-line experience by offering contextually relevant suggestions for subcommands, options, and arguments for hundreds of popular CLIs.
key_features: IDE-style autocompletion for terminal commands
Supports hundreds of popular CLIs (git, npm, docker, aws, etc.)
Contextually relevant suggestions for subcommands, options, and arguments
Declarative schema for defining completions ('completion specs')
Extensible with custom completion specs
Works with various terminals on macOS, Linux, and Windows
primary_use_case: Enhancing command-line productivity by providing intelligent autocompletion for terminal commands.
cover_image_prompt: A friendly, glowing guide holding a magical compass that points to different command-line tools like 'git', 'docker', and 'aws'. As the guide gestures, streams of relevant subcommands, options, and arguments flow from the compass, illuminating a dark, complex terminal interface and making it clear and organized. Subtle digital particles and small, stylized UI elements representing code suggestions float around the scene. The overall mood is helpful and empowering, with a clean, modern aesthetic. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with vibrant, glowing accents against a dark background.

name: nerdlog
description: Nerdlog: fast, remote-first, multi-host TUI log viewer with timeline histogram and no central server
readme_summary: Nerdlog is a fast, remote-first TUI log viewer designed for efficiently querying and visualizing logs from multiple hosts simultaneously. It requires no central server and performs log analysis on remote nodes, downloading only necessary data for display and timeline histograms.
key_features: Remote-first, multi-host TUI log viewer
No central server required
Log analysis performed on remote nodes
Interactive timeline histogram for visual insight
Efficient querying even on large log files
Gzipped data transfer for bandwidth saving
Supports arbitrary commands for SSH sessions (e.g., Teleport)
primary_use_case: Reading system logs from one or more remote Linux hosts efficiently, with a focus on web service backend logs, enabling quick filtering by time range and patterns.
cover_image_prompt: A skilled cartographer meticulously drawing a detailed map of a vast, interconnected network of islands, each island representing a remote server. Instead of traditional ink, the cartographer uses glowing streams of log data, which flow from each island onto a central parchment. A prominent, interactive timeline runs along the bottom of the map, highlighting significant events with glowing markers. Small, stylized UI elements resembling a TUI interface are subtly integrated into the cartographer's tools. The overall mood is one of focused exploration and clarity, with a clean, digital painting style and a color palette of deep blues and vibrant oranges.

name: Linkora
description: Local-first link organizer for Android and desktop with self-hosted sync.
readme_summary: Linkora is a local-first link organizer for Android and desktop that allows users to save, organize, and sync links across devices. It supports features like folders, tags, and self-hosted synchronization, along with a browser extension for easy saving.
key_features: Organize links with folders, subfolders, and tags
Multiple view layouts with AMOLED theme support
Highlight important links and archive old ones
Customize link names and auto-recognize images/titles
Share from other apps and add folders to Panels
Sort, search, import/export data (JSON, HTML) with auto-backups
Sync links across devices with optional self-hostable server
Browser extension for direct saving
primary_use_case: Organizing and synchronizing web links across multiple devices (Android and desktop) with an optional self-hosted sync server.
cover_image_prompt: A digital librarian meticulously organizing a vast, glowing library of interconnected web pages. The librarian is shown placing glowing links into organized shelves representing folders and tagging them with luminous icons. On one side, a tablet displays the Android interface, and on the other, a desktop monitor shows the desktop application, both seamlessly connected by ethereal data streams to a small, self-hosted server icon in the background. The overall scene is clean, modern, and futuristic, with a color palette of blues, purples, and subtle gold highlights, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style.
